deep.description: Route to deep-worker for heavy implementation
deep.template: Handle this task with thoroughness and precision.

quick.description: Route to light-orchestrator for simple tasks
quick.template: Handle this task quickly and efficiently.

ui.description: Route to ui-builder for frontend work
ui.template: Build or modify the UI as requested.

review.description: Route to reviewer for code review

plan.description: Route to planner for strategic planning
plan.template: Create a detailed plan for the following.

search.description: Route to librarian for external research
search.template: Research and find information about the following.

oracle.description: Route to oracle for deep code analysis and root cause debugging
oracle.template: Analyze and find the root cause of the following.

consult.description: Route to consultant for brainstorming and best-practice advice
consult.template: Provide options and advice for the following.

docs.description: Verify a library/API against current docs before coding (retrieval-first)
docs.template: Load the verify-with-docs skill. The task targets a specific or fast-moving library/framework/API. Do NOT answer from memory: identify the exact package + version in use, fetch or consult the current official docs (or a mounted `references` source), and report the exact, verified API signatures and usage the implementing agent should rely on. Cite source URLs/paths.

release.description: Prepare a tagged release using the git-release skill
release.template: Load the git-release skill and prepare a tagged release: draft release notes from merged PRs, propose a SemVer bump, and produce the gh release command.

commit.description: Stage changes and write a Conventional Commits message
commit.template:
Load the conventional-commits skill. Here is the current state:

Status:
!`git status --short`

Diff (staged + unstaged):
!`git diff HEAD`

Stage the relevant changes and write a single Conventional Commits message whose subject explains WHY the change was made from the user's perspective. Do not push.

learn.description: Persist durable, non-obvious project facts into AGENTS.md
learn.template: Review this session for durable, non-obvious facts about this codebase — conventions, gotchas, build/test commands, structural relationships — that would help future sessions. Append only genuinely reusable, long-lived facts (no task-specific or ephemeral notes, no secrets) to the appropriate section of AGENTS.md. Keep entries concise. If nothing qualifies, say so and change nothing.

rmslop.description: Remove AI slop and dead code from the current changes
rmslop.template:
Load the remove-deadcode skill. Review the current diff:

!`git diff HEAD`

Remove AI slop introduced by the changes: filler/boilerplate comments that restate the code, commented-out code, needless defensive wrappers, and any dead code that verification proves is unreferenced. Follow AGENTS.md Comment Discipline. Do not change behavior; verify build/tests still pass and report what you removed.

propose.description: Draft a spec-driven change proposal (proposal + specs + tasks) under openspec/changes/
propose.template: Load the spec-workflow skill and run its propose action. Research the existing code and any openspec/specs/ first, then scaffold openspec/changes/<change-id>/ with proposal.md, tasks.md, delta specs, and design.md only if warranted. Focus the proposal on WHY and WHAT, not HOW. Do not start implementing — stop after the artifacts are ready for review.

apply.description: Implement a spec-driven change by working through its tasks.md checklist
apply.template: Load the spec-workflow skill and run its apply action for the requested change under openspec/changes/. Read proposal.md, design.md, and the delta specs, then work tasks.md one unchecked item at a time, marking each `- [x]` as you finish. Pause and ask if a task is blocked or the design proves wrong (update the artifacts instead of diverging silently). Follow AGENTS.md and verify with any available build/tests.

archive.description: Archive a completed spec-driven change and fold its specs into the source of truth
archive.template: Load the spec-workflow skill and run its archive action for the requested completed change. Fold its delta specs into openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md (apply ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED/RENAMED), then move openspec/changes/<change-id>/ to openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<change-id>/. Confirm openspec/specs/ reflects reality before finishing.

codemap.description: Generate a structured hierarchical map of the codebase for quick orientation
codemap.template: Load the codemap skill and generate an annotated directory tree for this project. Build the raw tree (depth 3, excluding node_modules/.git/dist/build/.next/coverage), annotate key directories with one-line purpose descriptions, and add quick-reference metadata (language, framework, runtime, build/test commands). Keep the whole map under 200 lines. If .opencode/codemap.md exists and appears fresh, update it rather than overwriting.

simplify.description: Simplify code without changing behavior — reduce nesting, remove unnecessary abstraction, cut dead variables
simplify.template: Load the simplify skill. The user wants behavior-preserving simplification of specified code. Read the target file(s), apply the simplification patterns (early returns, inline single-use variables, remove single-caller functions, simplify conditionals), verify each change preserves behavior, and report what was simplified with before/after. Do not change public API signatures or remove error handling.

skill.description: Manage agent skills — search, preview, install, update, or publish
skill.template: Load the gh-skill skill. The user wants to manage agent skills. Use `gh skill` commands to search, preview, install, update, list, or publish skills. For installs targeting opencode, always pass `--agent opencode`. For local development, prefer `--scope project`. Pin to a ref for reproducibility.
